observations.recommendation: 3. For those found that collections were remote and could no longer be collectible, evaluate and provide an allowance on impairment and initiate the preparation of request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: There still exists dormant receivable accounts.

observations.recommendation: 3. The preparation and submission of the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with the guidelines provided by the GPPB particularly on the use of proper form and yet the timeline of its submission. Make use of the report as a tool for monitoring and assessing performance and in answering gaps and reform-related issues.
observations.reason: No preparation of PMR yet

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Management to implement strictly adhere to the guidelines and
procedures provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and
implement the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, especially those
unsubstantiated balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) to direct the GSO and Inventory
Committee to dispose the unserviceable properties and adhere in the guidelines and
procedures related to the disposal, as provided by the Handbook on Property and
Supply Management System; and (2) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to secure
necessary documents and draw journal entries related to the disposal of the
unserviceable properties.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) confirm the amount of payables
from the list of the identified creditors to determine which of the payables are still valid
obligation; (2) refund excess project funds to the respective source agency or
otherwise request for transfer to its General Fund, whichever is applicable, in
accordance with the terms of the MoA, and (3) to direct the Heads of implementing
departments in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to implement promptly the
corresponding PPAs funded by other NGAs and liquidate timely the amount
transferred.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) undertake critical processes in
assessing the budgets or allocation to include priority and procurement-and-
implementation ready development projects; (2) require the Municipal Development
Council to intensify monitoring and enforcing of implementation of budgeted
development projects to ensure completion within the target dates; (3) evaluate the
unimplemented PPAs as to its viability and feasibility of implementation, and reassess
its necessity for realignment if needed. (4) Henceforth, the Local Finance Committee to
have a periodic assessment of completed projects for timely reversion and
appropriation of any excess funds thereon.

We recommend the Management to (1) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
incorporate all Budget Realignments, if any, to SAAOB, FDP utilization and other 20%
LDF related reports for complete reporting, (2) prepare SAAOB of continuing
appropriations with inclusion of all PPAs fully utilized during the year and (3) prepare
20% of IRA utilization comprehensive encompassing all necessary details and update
the report on a quarterly basis as required for full disclosure policy; (4) direct the
Municipal Accountant to review and henceforth prepare SCBAA correctly and
completely and (5) to instruct both Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant
to review and reconcile budget reports of 20% LDF on periodic basis.

We recommended and Management agreed to review all available resources as early
as possible in order to formulate plan of action to be implemented throughout the year
and for the LGU to be prepared and can promptly respond to constituents in times of
calamities.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of splitting purchase
requisitions and/or contracts and to strictly comply with the existing regulations of the
revised IRR of RA 9184 in connection with the barangay governments procurement
activities.

We recommended and Management agreed to impose 100% compliance to GSIS
billings on loans by prioritizing the salary deductions of employees who have
outstanding loans. The Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) should make sure that before
approving the loan applied by a particular employee, his/her net take-home pay is
sufficient to cover the regular monthly amortization.

1. For the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to implement the proper conduct of inventory taking of all LGU owned Property, Plant and Equipment and preparation of Physical Inventory Plan guided by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to ascertain the existence, completeness and condition of all PPE accounts.
2. For the General Service Office, to promptly prepare and submit the Correct Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team for validation.

3. For the General Service Office and the Municipal Accounting Office, to prepare annual reconciliation of the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting records to establish the correct value of PPE at year end.
observations.reason: Existence of unreconciled beginning balance.

observations.recommendation: 4. For the General Service Office, to promptly prepare and submit the complete and comprehensive Report of Inventory & Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property to the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team for validation.

5. For the Municipal Accountant, to secure supporting documents and draw the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect recognition of depreciation expenses and adjustments to all the affected accounts. Henceforth, properly recognize annual depreciation of Property, Plant and Equipment based on the guidelines set under IPSAS 17 and other relevant COA issuances.
observations.reason: Existence of unreconciled beginning balance.

observations.recommendation: 6. For the Municipal Accounting Office together with the General Service Office and Inventory Committee, to properly implement one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, draw necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation and disclosures as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Existence of unreconciled beginning balance.

observations.recommendation: 1. For the Local Chief Executive to constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of Inventory of Municipality’s Inventories at least once a year. Upon completion of the count, prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the same to COA for verification and validation.

2. For the General Service Office to maintain Stock Cards (SCs) for each type of inventory to record all receipts, issuances and adjustments of supplies which shall become the basis for physical count and reconciliation of records.

3. For the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each type of supplies and inventory items, to record acquisitions, issuance and disposal and reconcile the same with the GSO records. Account the receipt, issuance, adjustments and the relevant reporting of Inventory accounts in accordance with Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units.
1. Exert diligent efforts to send demand letter to Business Chamber of Maasim, Inc. and LUPA MPC, Lumasal-Panang Coop and cause their collection.
2. Require written communication to document the comments and responses of the members who received the fund.
observations.reason: Management still on process in implementing the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: 1. Scrutinize the unsubstantiated bank accounts that appeared in the Cash in Bank – LCCA under trust fund, and effect the necessary adjusting entries.

2. Draw the necessary adjusting entries on the stale checks to restore the proper Cash in Bank balance. For the entries made on stale checks that were credited Prior Years’ Adjustment, identify the proper liability account and provide for adjusting entries. Revert the liability accounts only when it meets the conditions set forth under the reversion of unliquidated balances of accounts payable criteria set forth under Presidential Decree No. 1445.
3. Maintain cashbooks in all Cash in Bank Accounts of the LGU even if no transactions transpired for the year.
4. Regularly monitor the status of checks released and unreleased. Send written notice to the concerned payee regarding the existence of the checks at least a month before it becomes stale.

5. Return the original DVs and supporting documents of the staled checks to the Municipal Accountant who will prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to restore the amount of Cash in Bank and record the appropriate liability account.

1. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to properly and timely coordinate with the concerned offices of the schedules of official travels to ensure that documents and checks in settlement of obligations are timely drawn as prescribed under the Local Government Code. In case of temporary absence or incapacity of the said officials, these duties shall be devolved upon their immediate assistants. For the Municipal Treasurer to improve internal controls in the preparation of checks to ensure correctness and compliance with the prescribed procedures.
observations.reason: Occurrence of cancelled checks had been minimized

observations.recommendation: 1. For the Local Chief Executive to demand concerned departments in cash management for the evaluation of the effectiveness of controls in the monitoring Cash in Bank balances to identify procedural problems that resulted to the noted deficiencies and promulgate control activities that will promote operational efficiency.
2. For the Municipal Accountant to establish the validity for claim of reimbursement of initial deposit made to the accounts for DOE funded projects and prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

3. For the Municipal Treasurer to revisit the provisions on the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with DOE and confirm when can funds be actually downloaded. Further, make a representation with AGDB to revert the service charges debited to the account, otherwise, be held accountable for the incurrence of the said expenses.

1. We recommended and management concurred that the Municipal Government shall formulate its development programs, projects and activities on the funded under 20% Development Fund with a specific, measurable, attainable, realistic and time-bounded attributes of a good plans and budget as prescribed by the LGC and Budget Operations Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of implementing the recommendation

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommended and likewise concurred by management that for the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and Local Finance Council (LFC) on the to assess all savings from completed projects and implementability of projects that remained outstanding for several years and consider its reversion and re-appropriation to other priority development projects in compliance with the guidelines on the utilization of the fund.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of implementing the recommendation

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommended and Management concurred to identify the cause of non-implementation of the identified PPAs, evaluate its relevance to the current situation and its alignment with national government’s initiative and henceforth undertake critical, urgent, and appropriate disaster response aid and measures to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of implementing the recommendation

observations.recommendation: 1. Direct the MSWDO Head to enhance the internal control and employ additional mechanisms in the distribution of financial aids to avoid the incurrence of delays in the future releases and payouts.

2. Direct the Municipal Accounting Office Head to remind the Special Disbursing Officers (SDOs) of their obligation to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period to avoid the adverse effects of delays.
3. Ensure that the inter-agency funds' utilization and corresponding liquidation adhere to the prescribed guidelines.

1. For the Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance Council (LFC), to ensure that all appropriations are properly supported with plans. Harmonize local plans with investment programs, budget and expenditure management procedures.

2. For the Local Development Council (LDC) to submit to this Office the approved Supplemental Investment Program for the appropriation of COVID-19 related programs, projects and activities (PPAs) in compliance with DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020, and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2 dated March 30, 2020.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of implementing the recommendation

observations.recommendation: 3. Henceforth, whenever the Municipality receives a Fund that requires the creation of Special Account in the General Fund, the Local Chief Executive should ensure that the rules on its creation which includes among others the enactment of an ordinance and maintenance of separate subsidiary ledgers are duly complied with.

1. For the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan to stop granting bonuses and incentives that are devoid of legal basis and exceeding the prescribed rates and refrain from approving disbursements that are tainted with irregularities.

2. For the Municipal Accountant together with Municipal Treasurer to ensure that disbursements against public funds are duly supported with complete and relevant documents before passing in pre-audit and processing the payments.

3. For the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements for the salary/wage and other expenses in the engagement of Contract of Service and Job Order Workers are charged and recorded in the proper expense accounts under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses.

4. For the Human Resource Management Office to ensure that benefits and other compensation of employees and workers are properly deducted and withheld with taxes as prescribed under NIRC and RA 10963.

1. Deposit intact all collections including those turned over by the deputized collectors with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day and avoid keeping large amount of cash in the office vault.

2. Deputized collectors of the Municipality shall accomplish Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) at the close of each business day and turn over intact the cash collections to Municipal Treasurer or the designated liquidating officer. In the case of collectors assigned in the field, including deputized barangay collectors for collections made for the municipality, where travel time from their places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00.
3. Henceforth, hire additional employees under the MTO to address the gaps in the manpower requirements to cater all collections from taxpayers.

1. Ensure that all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year. For the operating expenses, management should see to it that cash advances granted is tantamount to the activity/ies that can be accomplished within the year. Unused cash advances for travels and seminars for the year should be returned or refunded to the collecting officer at year-end.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of implementing the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: 2. Review the dormant or long outstanding cash advances, if found the collection is futile despite demand and/or efforts exerted, prepare the request for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances pursuant to COA 2016-005 with supporting documents.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of implementing the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the source agencies and reconcile the non-moving balances included in the Due to NGAs and Due to other LGUs to reflect the accurate amount of the liability accounts. Make a representation on the letter for confirmation to the source agencies for the unliquidated balances for proper reconciliation and adjustments in the books if warranted and/or immediate liquidation.

2. For the Municipal Engineering Office to revisit the provisions under the Annex “E” of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 and ensure that approval on the contract on the time extension is properly done and documented implementing the recommendation.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of implementing the recommendation

observations.recommendation: 3. For the BAC together with the General Service Office to ensure clarity in the contracts between the Supplier and LGU and prescribed forms are properly filled out.
1. Henceforth, the Municipality shall include the necessary disclosures in the notes of the comparative financial statements.

1. The Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance Council (LFC), to continue their commitment to adhere to the guidelines set on the harmonization of the local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting and expenditure management.

1. Henceforth, lumpsum appropriations are avoided, instead, ensure that local budget operationalizes approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.

2. The Local Budget Office to provide reconciliation and justification and take up adjustments, if necessary, on the noted deviations in the budget report. Henceforth, strengthen internal controls on the review and monitoring budget related records and reports to ensure its completeness, accuracy and reliability

1. The timely preparation and submission of the end-users of PPMPs and consolidation of the BAC Secretariat of APP in accordance with the provisions of GPRA using the suggested format of the GPPB duly posted in its website.
observations.reason: Management still on the process in implementing the recommendation

observations.recommendation: 2. The formulation of complete and approved procurement plans consistent with the approved budget. The different Department Heads should ensure that the PPMPs they prepare is complete and consistent with their approved budget. Accordingly, the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office, should ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipality’s Approved Annual Budget.

1. Make representations with the bank officials to finally determine the status of the bank balance appearing in the LGU books. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances based on the result of the representation made.
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the complete bank reconciliation statements for all depository accounts. Likewise, all should be submitted as at year-end.

1. Require the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the source agencies and reconcile the non-moving balances included in the Due to NGAs and Due to other LGUs to reflect the accurate amount of the liability accounts. Make a representation on the letter for confirmation to the source agencies for the unliquidated balances for proper reconciliation and adjustments in the books if warranted and/or immediate liquidation.

1. We recommend to find measures to address and promote efficiency of project implementation. Assign infrastructure projects to the Municipal Engineering Office which have sufficient skilled personnel complement to address the deficiency of skilled personnel concerning infrastructure projects.
1. Determine and dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines.
observations.reason: Management still on the process in implementing the recommendation

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended and management agreed to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and start the process of writing off the accounts in conformity with COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.reason: Management still on the process in implementing the recommendation

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended and management agreed to expedite the construction of the sanitary landfill in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure protection of public health and the environment.

1. We recommended and management agreed to optimally utilize the available resources to achieve maximum disaster preparedness. In cases of reshuffling or employee separation, ensure proper turnover of tasks and PPAs to make certain the implementation / execution of the same.

1. We recommended that management give similar effort in the prosecution of NGAs and locally funded projects. Hence, the LGU should maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and management
concurred on the following, some of which were reiteration of the prior years’ audit
recommendations:

a. For the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to implement
the proper conduct of inventory taking of all LGU owned Property, Plant and
Equipment and preparation of Physical Inventory Plan guided by COA Circular
No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to ascertain the existence, completeness and
condition of all PPE accounts;

b. For the General Service Office, to promptly prepare and submit the Correct
Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Municipal
Accountant and the Audit Team for validation;

c. For the General Service Office and the Municipal Accounting Office, to prepare
annual reconciliation of the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting
records to establish the correct value of PPE at year end;

d. For the General Service Office, to promptly prepare and submit the complete and
comprehensive Report of Inventory & Inspection Report on Unserviceable
Property to the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team for validation;

e. For the Municipal Accountant, to secure supporting documents and draw the
necessary adjustments in the books to reflect recognition of depreciation expenses
and adjustments to all the affected accounts. Henceforth, properly recognize annual
depreciation of Property, Plant and Equipment based on the guidelines set under
IPSAS 17 and other relevant COA issuances; and

f. For the Municipal Accounting Office together with the General Service Office
and Inventory Committee, to properly implement one-time cleansing of PPE
account balances, draw necessary accounting entries, measurements,
presentation and disclosures as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.

a. For the Local Chief Executive to constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct
physical count of all Municipality’s Inventories at least once a year. Upon
completion of the count, prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) and submit the same to COA for verification and validation;

b. For the General Service Office to maintain Stock Cards (SCs) for each type of
inventory to record all receipts, issuances and adjustments of supplies which shall
become the basis for physical count and reconciliation of records;

c. For the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC)
for each type of supplies and inventory items, to record acquisitions, issuance and
disposal and reconcile the same with the GSO records. Account the receipt,
issuance, adjustments and the relevant reporting of Inventory accounts in
accordance with Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government
Units.

We recommended and management concurred on the following:

a. Exert diligent efforts to send demand letter to Business Chamber of Maasim, Inc.
and LUPA MPC, Lumasal-Panang Coop and cause their collection;

b. Require written communication to document the comments and responses of the
members who received the fund; and

c. For those found that collections were remote and could no longer be collectible,
evaluate and provide an allowance on impairment and initiate the preparation of
request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the accounts in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

For the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Scrutinize the unsubstantiated bank accounts that appeared in the Cash in Bank
– LCCA under trust fund, and effect the necessary adjusting entries;
b) Draw the necessary adjusting entries on the stale checks to restore the proper
Cash in Bank- LCCA balance. For the entries made on stale checks that were
credited Prior Years’ Adjustment, identify the proper liability account and
provide for adjusting entries. Revert the liability accounts only when it meets the
conditions set forth under the reversion of unliquidated balances of accounts
payable criteria set forth under Presidential Decree No. 1445;
For the Municipal Treasurer to:
a) Maintain cashbooks in all Cash in Bank Accounts of the LGU even if no
transactions transpired for the year;
b) Regularly monitor the status of checks released and unreleased. Send written
notice to the concerned payee regarding the existence of the checks at least a
month before it becomes stale; and
c) Return the original DVs and supporting documents of the staled checks to the
Municipal Accountant who will prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
restore the amount of Cash in Bank and record the appropriate liability account.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and management concurred for the
Local Chief Executive to properly and timely coordinate with the concerned offices of
the schedules of official travels to ensure that documents and checks in settlement of
obligations are timely drawn as prescribed under the Local Government Code. In case
of temporary absence or incapacity of the said officials, these duties shall be devolved
upon their immediate assistants. For the Municipal Treasurer to improve internal
controls in the preparation of checks to ensure correctness and compliance with the
prescribed procedures.

a. For the Local Chief Executive to demand concerned departments in cash
management for the evaluation of the effectiveness of controls in the monitoring
Cash in Bank balances to identify procedural problems that resulted to the noted
deficiencies and promulgate control activities that will promote operational
efficiency;

b. For the Municipal Accountant to establish the validity for claim of
reimbursement of initial deposit made to the accounts for DOE funded projects
and prepare the necessary adjusting entries; and

c. For the Municipal Treasurer to revisit the provisions on the Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) with DOE and confirm when can funds be actually
downloaded. Further, make a representation with AGDB to revert the service
charges debited to the account, otherwise, be held accountable for the incurrence
of the said expenses.

We recommended and management concurred that the Municipal Government shall
formulate its development programs, projects and activities funded under 20%
Development Fund with a specific, measurable, attainable, realistic and time-
bounded attributes of a good plans and budget as prescribed by the LGC and Budget
Operations Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and likewise concurred by management that for the Municipal
Development Council (MDC) and Local Finance Council (LFC) to assess all savings from
completed projects and implementability of projects that remained outstanding for
several years and consider its reversion and re-appropriation to other priority
development projects in compliance with the guidelines on the utilization of the fund.

We recommended and Management concurred to identify the cause of
non-implementation of the identified PPAS, evaluate its relevance to the current
situation and its alignment with national government’s initiative and henceforth
undertake critical, urgent, and appropriate disaster response aid and measures to
curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19.

a. Direct the MSWDO Head to enhance the internal control and employ additional
mechanisms in the distribution of financial aids to avoid the incurrence of delays
in the future releases and payouts;

b. Direct the Municipal Accounting Office Head to remind the Special Disbursing
Officers (SDOs) of their obligation to liquidate their cash advances within the
prescribed period to avoid the adverse effects of delays; and

c. Ensure that the inter-agency funds' utilization and corresponding liquidation
adhere to the prescribed guidelines.

a. For the Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance
Council (LFC), to ensure that all appropriations are properly supported with
plans. Harmonize local plans with investment programs, budget and expenditure
management procedures;

b. For the Local Development Council (LDC) to submit to this Office the approved
Supplemental Investment Program for the appropriation of COVID-19 related
programs, projects and activities (PPAs) in compliance with DBM LBC No. 125
dated April 7, 2020, and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2 dated March 30, 2020; and

c. Henceforth, whenever the Municipality receives a Fund that requires the creation
of Special Account in the General Fund, the Local Chief Executive should ensure
that the rules on its creation which includes among others the enactment of an
ordinance and maintenance of separate subsidiary ledgers are duly complied
with.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management concurred on the
following:

a) For the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan to stop granting
bonuses and incentives that are devoid of legal basis and exceeding the
prescribed rates and refrain from approving disbursements that are tainted
with irregularities;

b) For the Municipal Accountant together with Municipal Treasurer to ensure that
disbursements against public funds are duly supported with complete and relevant
documents before passing in pre-audit and processing the payments;

c) For the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements for the
salary/wage and other expenses in the engagement of Contract of Service and
Job Order Workers are charged and recorded in the proper expense accounts
under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses; and

d) For the Human Resource Management Office to ensure that benefits and
other compensation of employees and workers are properly deducted and
withheld with taxes as prescribed under NIRC and RA 10963.

We recommended and Management concurred on the following:
a. For the Local Chief Executive to cause the establishment of a written internal guidelines or procedures in the management of cash to address the above deficiencies and gaps, and ensure that they will be fully disseminated to and implemented by the concerned offices of the Municipal Government.
b. For the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and exert the efforts in gathering the necessary supporting documents to record the valid accounts under SEF and Trust Fund.
c. For the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and exert the efforts in gathering the necessary supporting documents to record the valid accounts under SEF and Trust Fund.
d. For the Municipal Treasurer to update cash books and record the valid reconciling entries as evidenced by the journal entry vouchers and bank reconciliation statements received from the Accounting Office as of December 31, 2019 and in the succeeding periods.
e. For the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Cashbook for all Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account to compare balances with the subsidiary ledger with past years. Even if no transaction transpired during the year, the preparation and updating of the cashbook should still be continued otherwise it will expose the fund into the risk of non-recording and non-reconciliation thereby affecting the accuracy and completeness of financial records and reports. Further, if the maintenance of a particular bank account is no longer necessary or purpose has already been achieved, it is required to return the excess fund to the source agencies and close the account to avoid dormancy fees and unnecessary bank charges in the future.

observations.recommendation:
a. Deposit intact all collections including those turned over by the deputized collectors with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day and avoid keeping large amount of cash in the office vault.
b. Deputized collectors of the Municipality shall accomplish Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) at the close of each business day and turn over intact collections to the Municipal Treasurer or the designated liquidating officer. In the case of barangay collectors, including deputized barangay collectors for collections were not fully issued.

turnover intact to the

observations.reason: Sample collections and deposit was examined and disclosed that collections were not turnover intact; while those deputized barangay collectors, one stub of AF were fully issued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management concurred on the following:
a. Ensure that all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year. For the operating expenses, management should see to it that cash advances granted is tantamount to the activity/ies that can be accomplished within the year. Unused cash advances for travels and seminars for the year should be returned or refunded to the collecting officer at year-end.
b. Ensure that cash advances shall only be given for a legally authorized specific purpose and adequately supported with relevant supporting documents thereby casting doubt on the validity and propriety of the financial transaction and its recording.
c. Review the dormant or long outstanding cash advances, if found the collection is futile despite demand and/or efforts exerted, prepare the request for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances pursuant to COA 2016-005 with proper supporting documents;
d. Revisit the provisions on supporting documents on the grants and liquidations of cash advances and strictly adhere thereto; and
e. Stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees until the previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled.
f. Strictly deny the certificate of clearance to employees with unliquidated cash advances/shortages who are retiring, resigning and or seeking reassignment. Send demand letters to separated employees to liquidate or refund the cash advance.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2019
agreed to the following:
For the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure that all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year.
For the Municipal Treasurer to:
Revisit the provisions on supporting documents on the grants and liquidations of cash advances and strictly adhere thereto.

AAR 2019
concurred on the following:
For the Local Chief Executive to create Inventory Committee, its proper documentation and assignment of personnel to conduct annual physical inventory/inventory taking of its Property, Plant and Equipment.
For the General Service Office to prepare and submit the Correct Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team for validation.
For the General Service Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare annual reconciliation of the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting records.
For the Municipal Accounting Office to obtain necessary data from Municipal Engineering Office, then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for Local Road Network and provide depreciation separately to each component of the road network.

AAR 2019
agreed on the following:
The Local Treasurer, together with the Local Accountant to undertake comprehensive Cash Analysis and ensure that funds are actually available prior to certifying Statement of Revenue/Income/Savings as fund source of supplemental budgets.
The Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance Council (LFC), to continue their commitment to adhere to the guidelines on the harmonization of the local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting and expenditure management.
The Local Budget Office to provide reconciliation and justification and take up adjustments if necessary on noted deviations in the budget report.
The Municipal Engineering Office to revisit the provisions under Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184.
The BAC together with the GSO to ensure clarity in the contracts.

observations.recommendation:
Thus, we recommended that the Local Treasurer, together with the Local Accountant to undertake comprehensive Cash Analysis and ensure that funds are actually available prior to certifying Statement of Revenue/Income/Savings as fund source of supplemental budgets.
The Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance Council (LFC), to continue their commitment to adhere to the guidelines on the harmonization of the local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting and expenditure management. Henceforth, lumpsum appropriations devoid of safeguards are avoided.
The Local Budget Office to provide reconciliation and justification and take up adjustments if necessary on noted deviations in the budget report. Henceforth, the different Department Heads should ensure that the PPMPs they prepare is complete and consistent with their approved budget. Accordingly, the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office, should ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipality’s Approved Annual Budget.
The Local Budget Office to provide reconciliation and justification and take up adjustments if necessary on noted deviations in the budget report. Henceforth, the different Department Heads should ensure that the PPMPs they prepare is complete and consistent with their approved budget. Accordingly, the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office, should ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipality’s Approved Annual Budget.
The Local Budget Office to provide reconciliation and justification and take up adjustments if necessary on noted deviations in the budget report. Henceforth, the different Department Heads should ensure that the PPMPs they prepare is complete and consistent with their approved budget. Accordingly, the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office, should ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipality’s Approved Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2019
The Local Treasurer, together with the Local Accountant to undertake comprehensive Cash Analysis and ensure that funds are actually available prior to certifying Statement of Revenue/Income/Savings as fund source of supplemental budgets.
The Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance Council (LFC), to continue their commitment to adhere to the guidelines on the harmonization of the local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting and expenditure management.
The Local Budget Office to provide reconciliation and justification and take up adjustments if necessary on noted deviations in the budget report.
observations.reason: No reconciliation and justification were provided. Differences in balances as shown in Status of Appropriation, Allotment, Obligations and Balances and the Appropriation Ordinances and per mathematical computation at a net amount of ₱2,183,566.26 and ₱243,270.07 respectively affects the accuracy and reliability of the reported balances in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
Therefore, we recommended and Management agreed compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Reform Act, its IRR and the relevant issuances of the GPPB on the following:
a. The Annual Procurement Plan (APP) is not consistent with the approved budget. The timely preparation and submission of the end-users duly approved PPMPs and consolidation of the APP in accordance with the Government Procurement Reform Act (GPRA) using the suggested format of the GPPB duly posted in its website.
b. The formulation of complete and approved procurement plans consistent with the approved budget. The different Department Heads should ensure that the PPMPs they prepare is complete and consistent with their approved budget. Accordingly, the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office, should ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipality’s Approved Annual Budget.
c. The preparation and submission of the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with the guidelines provided by the GPPB particularly on the use of proper form and the timeline of its submission. Make use of the report as a tool for monitoring and assessing performance and in answering gaps and reform-related issues.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2019
agreed compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Reform Act, its IRR and the relevant issuances of the GPPB. The BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office, shall ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipality’s Approved Annual Budget.

AAR 2019
Make representations with the bank officials to finally determine the status of the bank balance appearing in the LGU books. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct cash balance based on the result of the representation made.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the complete bank reconciliation statements for all depository accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the source agencies and reconcile the non-moving balances included in the Due to NGAs and Due to other LGUs to reflect the accurate amount of the liability accounts. Make a representation with the source agencies and reconcile the non-moving balances included in the Due to NGAs and Due to other LGUs to reflect the accurate amount of the liability accounts. b. For the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to be attached in the disbursement vouchers upon submission.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016-2018
Require the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the source agencies and reconcile the non-moving balances included in the Due to NGAs and Due to other LGUs to reflect the accurate amount of the liability accounts.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts still exists.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that management give similar effort in the prosecution of NGAs and locally funded projects. Hence, the LGU should maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2016-2017
agreed to the following:
The LGU shall optimize available resources and assign infrastructure projects to the Municipal Engineering Office which have sufficient skilled personnel complement to address the deficiency of skilled personnel concerning infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Some prior years projects were still not implemented

AAR 2012-2018
agreed on the following:
The General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to conduct the physical count, prepare IIRUP and dispose the unserviceable items.
Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and start the process of writing off the accounts in conformity with COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2012-2018
agreed to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and start the process of writing off the accounts in conformity with COA Circular 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to expedite the construction of the sanitary landfill in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure protection of public health and the environment.

AAR 2017
agreed and the LGU to expedite the construction of the sanitary landfill in accordance with R.A. 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to optimally utilize the available resources to achieve maximum disaster preparedness. In cases of reshuffling or employee separation, ensure proper turnover of tasks and PPAs to make certain the implementation / execution of the same.

AAR 2016-2017
agreed to the following:
The LGU shall maximize utilization of the LDRRMF and ensure proper turnover of tasks.

observations.recommendation: Thus, we recommended and management agreed that the Local Development Council, as end-user, to prepare and submit their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to the BAC Secretariat for all budgetary items that requires undertaking of procurement in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR; the LGU should maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015-2017
agreed that the Local Development Council to prepare and submit their PPMPs to the BAC Secretariat and to maximize utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Some prior years projects were still not implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Development Council shall assess all savings from completed projects and implementability of projects that remained outstanding for several years and consider its reversion and re-appropriation to other priority development projects in compliance with the guidelines on the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
agreed to assess all savings from completed projects and implementability of projects that remained outstanding for several years and consider its reversion and re-appropriation to other priority development projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to undertake one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and properly recognize depreciation and make necessary disclosures as per IPSAS and COA circulars.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2012-2019
Management agreed to implement one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and to recognize depreciation and make necessary disclosures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance Council (LFC), to ensure that all appropriations are properly supported with plans. Harmonize local plans with investment programs, budget and expenditure management procedures.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2019
Management agreed and to harmonize plans and budgets and to submit the necessary SIPs.

observations.recommendation:
a. For the Local Chief Executive to cause the establishment of a written internal
guidelines or procedures in the management of cash to address the above
deficiencies and gaps, and ensure that they will be fully disseminated to and
implemented by the concerned offices of the Municipal Government.

b. For the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No.
96-011 and exert efforts in gathering the necessary supporting documents to
record the long unsettled/unadjusted valid reconciling items as reflected in the
Bank Reconciliation Statements. After the thorough tracing of the non-existing
accounts, adjust these bank accounts for correct cash balance at year-end.

c. For the Municipal Treasurer to update cash books and record the valid
reconciling entries as evidenced by the journal entry vouchers and bank
reconciliation statements received from the Accounting Office as of December
31, 2019 and in the succeeding periods.

d. For the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Cashbook for all Cash in Bank – Local
Currency Current Account to compare balances with the subsidiary ledger
with accounting office periodically.

e. For the Municipal Treasurer to monitor regularly the cash balances and
transactions to determine proper use of the accounts and prevent charges per
BSP regulations for dormant accounts.

a. Deposit intact all collections including those turned over by the deputized
collectors with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next
banking day and avoid keeping large amount of cash in the office vault.

b. Deputized collectors of the Municipality shall accomplish Report of Collections
and Deposits (RCD) at the close of each business day and turn over intact the
cash collections to Municipal Treasurer or the designated liquidating officer. In
the case of collectors assigned in the field, including deputized barangay
collectors for collections made for the municipality, where travel time from
their places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day,
turnover of collections shall be made at least once a week or as soon as the
collections reach ₱5,000.00.

c. Henceforth, hire additional employees under the MTO to address the gaps in the
manpower requirements to cater all collections from taxpayers.

a. Ensure that all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year. For
the operating expenses, management should see to it that cash advances granted is
tantamount to the activity/ies that can be accomplished within the year. Unused
cash advances for travels and seminars for the year should be returned or
refunded to the collecting officer at year-end.

b. Ensure that cash advances shall only be given for a legally authorized specific
purpose and adequately supported with relevant supporting documents;

c. Review the dormant or long outstanding cash advances, if found the collection is
futile despite demand and/or efforts exerted, prepare the request for authority to
write-off the unliquidated cash advances pursuant to COA 2016-005 with proper
supporting documents;

d. Revisit the provisions on supporting documents on the grants and liquidations of
cash advances and strictly adhere thereto; and

e. Stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees until the
previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled.

a. For the Local Chief Executive to institutionalize the creation of Inventory
Committee, its proper documentation and assignment of personnel to conduct
annual physical inventory/inventory taking of its Property Plant and Equipment.

b. For the General Service Office to promptly prepare and submit the Correct
Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Municipal
Accountant and the Audit Team for validation.

c. For the General Service Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to annually
reconcile the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting records to
establish the correct value of PPE at year end.

d. For the Municipal Accounting Office to obtain necessary data from Municipal
Engineering Office, then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for Local
Road Network, provide depreciation separately to each component of the road
network, submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation, and
disclose the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in
accordance with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

e. For the Municipal Accountant to secure supporting documents and draw the
necessary adjustments in the books to reflect recognition of depreciation expenses
and adjustments to all the affected accounts. Henceforth, properly recognize
annual depreciation of Property, Plant and Equipment based on the guidelines set
under IPSAS 17 and other relevant COA issuances.

f. For the management to revisit the provisions of IPSAS 17 on the required
disclosures for each class of property, plant and equipment recognized in the
financial statements and properly reflect the same on the Notes to Financial
Statements.

a. For the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the idle/long-outstanding
balances of Due to Other NGAs accounts. Make a representation letter for
confirmation to the source agencies for the unliquidated balances for proper
reconciliation and adjustments in the books if warranted and/or immediate
liquidation.

b. For the Accounting Office to maintain and update constantly the Subsidiary
Ledgers pursuant to COA Circular 2020-004, wherein the Local Government
Units should use the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs prescribing the
adaptation of IPSAS.

c. For the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure the completeness and propriety
of the supporting documents to be attached in the disbursement vouchers upon
submission.

d. For the Municipal Engineering Office to revisit the provisions under the Annex
“E” of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 and ensure that approval on the contract time
extension is properly done and documented.

e. For the BAC together with the General Service Office to ensure clarity in the
contracts between the Supplier and LGU and prescribed forms are properly
filled out.

Thus, we recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive, through the Local School Board to direct the Municipal Accountant to
include the necessary disclosures in the notes of the reasons of the material differences
in the comparative financial statements;

a. The Local Treasurer, together with the Local Accountant to undertake
comprehensive Cash Analysis and ensure that funds are actually available prior
to certifying Statement of Revenue/Income/Savings as fund source of
supplemental budgets.

b. The Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance
Council (LFC), to continue their commitment to adhere to the guidelines set on
the harmonization of the local planning, investment programming, revenue
administration, budgeting and expenditure management.

c. Henceforth, lumpsum appropriations are avoided, instead, ensure that local
budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains
identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.

d. The Local Budget Office to provide reconciliation and justification and take up
adjustments if necessary on the noted deviations in the budget report.

Henceforth, strengthen internal controls on the review and monitoring budget
related records and reports to ensure its completeness, accuracy and reliability.

Therefore, we recommended and Management agreed that the Local School
Board, as end-user, to prepare and submit their Project Procurement Management
Plan (PPMP) to the BAC Secretariat for all budgetary items that requires undertaking
of procurement in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR and other
issuances of the GPPB; (SEF)

a. The timely preparation and submission of the end-users of PPMPs and
consolidation of the BAC Secretariat of APP in accordance with the provisions
of GPRA using the suggested format of the GPPB duly posted in its website.

b. The formulation of complete and approved procurement plans consistent with
the approved budget. The different Department Heads should ensure that the
PPMPs they prepare is complete and consistent with their approved budget.
Accordingly, the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget
Office, should ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipality’s
Approved Annual Budget.

c. The preparation and submission of the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR)
in accordance with the guidelines provided by the GPPB particularly on the use
of proper form and the timeline of its submission. Make use of the report as a
tool for monitoring and assessing performance and in answering gaps and
reform-related issues.

We recommend the following: a. Require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances granted to officers and employees at year end. Observe strictly the rules and regulations regarding the granting and liquidation of cash advances. b. Strictly deny the certificate of clearance to employees with outstanding unliquidated cash advances/shortages who are retiring, resigning and or seeking reassignment. Send demand letters to separated employees to liquidate or refund the cash advance.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued. But to date no liquidation were made. Likewise, there were former employees which advance were found futile. These advances were considered as long outstanding unliquidated cash advances. Meanwhile, same observation were reiterated in the part II of this AAR.
observations.reason: Some of the unliquidated cash advances were granted to previous auditors. Demand letters were issued. But to date no liquidation were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: a. Make representations with the bank officials to finally determine the status of the bank balance appearing in the LGU books. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance based on the result of the representation made; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the complete bank reconciliation statements for all depository accounts. Likewise, all should be submitted as at year-end. c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to book the valid reconciling items as reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and should be supported by valid documents. And make adjustment on the stale checks to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Documented communication with the AGDB revealed that bank balances were not existed in the bank’s records. Thus, adjustments are to be implement in the succeeding year. Only few BRS were not prepared at year end.
observations.reason: Only few BRS were not prepared at year end; adjustments to derecognize unsupported bank balances to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the guidelines on the remittance of the withheld taxes within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made as required.

We recommend the following: a. Exhaust alternative measures in tracing the source of the PDAF and remit it to the National Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court decision; b. Require the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the source agencies to reconcile the non-moving balances included in the Due to NGAs and Due to other LGUs to reflect the accurate amount of the liability accounts. c. Rectify the previous entry made to appropriately recognize the proper accounts by debiting the Due to NGAs and crediting the Due to Other Funds.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts still observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to ensure that PPAs under the 20% LDF shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the municipal government and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure. Revisit the JMC 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
We recommend for the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages on payments to projects not completed within the stipulated contract period.
We recommend for the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries to reclassify into the proper accounts which pertain to the on-going infrastructure projects.

We recommend to find measures to address and promote efficiency of project implementation. Assign infrastructure projects to the Municipal Engineering Office which have sufficient skilled personnel complement to address the deficiency of skilled personnel concerning infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Based on the SAAOB, some PPAs were not implemented during the year.
observations.reason: Some prior years projects were still not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: a. Direct the Municipal General Services Officer to locate documents pertinent to the LGU properties preparatory to the conduct of the mandatory physical inventory; b. Determine and dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines; c. Provide depreciation for the Land Improvements and Infrastructure Assets; d. Require the GSO to revalidate the physical count of the properties and reconcile with the records of the MAO amounting to P52,563,201.12. e. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: MAO started to reconcile with GSO; some records being gathered; lapsing schedule to be provided; adjustments to be made.
observations.reason: Still in the process of reconciling with the MAO; gathering available records for the PPE that do not have property cards; certain adjustments not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and management agreed to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and start the process of writing off the accounts in conformity with COA Circular 2016-005.
We recommended and management agreed to expedite the construction of the sanitary landfill in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure protection of public health and the environment.
observations.reason: On-going project

In cases of reshuffling or employee separation, ensure proper turnover of tasks and PPAs to make certain the implementation / execution of the same.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant remit to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized balance amounting to P400,000.00 in compliance to the Supreme Court Decision.
observations.managementAction: Request Letter will be sent to LBP Koronadal.
observations.reason: To gather further information.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to the following:

a. Require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances granted to officers
and employees at year end. Observe strictly the rules and regulations regarding the
granting and liquidation of cash advances.

b. Strictly deny the certificate of clearance to employees with unliquidated cash
advances/shortages who are retiring, resigning and or seeking reassignment. Send
demand letters to separated employees to liquidate or refund the cash advances.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

a. Make representations with the bank officials to finally determine the status of the bank
balance appearing in the LGU books. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the
correct cash balances based on the result of the representation made;

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the complete bank
reconciliation statements for all depository accounts. Likewise, all should be
submitted as at year-end.

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to book the valid reconciling items as reflected in
the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and should be supported by valid
documents. And make adjustment on the stale checks to reflect the correct balance
of the affected accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

a. Require the GSO to revalidate the physical count of the properties and reconcile
with the records of the MAO amounting to P52,563,201.12.

b. Require the MAO to effect the appropriate and necessary adjustments in the
accounting records.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant strictly
comply with the guidelines on the remittance of the withheld taxes within ten (10) days
following the end of the month the withholding was made as required.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

a. Exhaust alternative measures in tracing the source of the PDAF and remit it to the National
Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court decision;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the source agencies and
reconcile the non-moving balances included in the Due to NGAs and Due to other
LGUs to reflect the accurate amount of the liability accounts.

c. Rectify the previous entry made to appropriately recognize the proper accounts by
debiting the Due to NGAs and crediting the Due to Other Funds.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to impose
liquidated damages on payments to projects which were not completed within stipulated
date of completion. An audit action of disallowance will be serve.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make
adjusting entries to reclassify into the proper accounts which pertain to the on-going
infrastructure projects.

We recommended and management agreed to find measures to address and
promote efficiency of project implementation. Assign infrastructure projects to the
Municipal Engineering Office which have sufficient skilled personnel complement to
address the deficiency of skilled personnel concerning infrastructure projects.

observations.reason: Still in the process of gathering available records for the PPE that do not have property cards, and property ledger.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and start the process of writing off the accounts in conformity with COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents to justify the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should impose substantial amount compliance to each of the municipal GSIS billings on loans through prioritization in the salary deductions of employees who have outstanding loans. The Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) should make sure that before approving the loan applied by a particular employee, his/her net take home pay is sufficient to cover the regular monthly amortization.
We recommended and management agreed to revisit the provisions of the above cited guidelines. Describe appropriately the projects and activities to qualify under the guidelines.

We recommended and management agreed to prepare the inventory list of items procured and/or donated for DRRM and submit the same to COA and Municipal Accounting Office using the sample forms in COA Circular 2014-002 Annexes P-1 and P-2.
We recommended and they agreed that adjustment should be made on the stale checks as of December 31, 2017 checks to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.
We recommended and management agreed to optimally utilize the available resources to achieve maximum disaster preparedness.
observations.reason: Delays in implementation were still observed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management give similar effort in the prosecution of NGAs and locally funded projects.
observations.reason: implementation were still observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU stop incurring expenses taken from the LDRRMF which are not related to disaster mitigation prevention and preparedness.

We recommend and reiterate that the LGU maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided. Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.

Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and

observations.recommendation: The Honorable Mayor is enjoined to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office reestablish and reconstruct the records based on available documents. Exercise diligent effort so as to enforced collections from beneficiaries/creditors. Institute administrative remedies for failure to settle the accounts after due notice or demand. Furthermore, unavailability of some documents and inadequacy of the accounting records makes the collection from NGO'S/PO's even more difficult.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant remit to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized balance of PDAF amounting to P400,000.00 from year 2014.
observations.managementAction: Request Letter was sent to LBP representative to determine the compliance to the Supreme Court Decision. PDAF. was it was a fund transfer from a bank in Koronadal City.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant remit to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized balance of PDAF amounting to P400,000.00 from year 2014.
observations.managementAction: Request Letter was sent to LBP representative to determine the compliance to the Supreme Court Decision.
observations.reason: Upon follow up, the only info they have was it was a fund transfer from a bank in Koronadal City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to the following:
a. Require the MAO and GSO to maintain sufficient property ledger cards and
property stock cards, respectively, as control records to facilitate the regular
reconciliation of records with MAO;
b. Determine and dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties in
accordance with the guidelines;
c. Provide depreciation for the Land Improvements and Infrastructure
Assets; and
d. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to conduct regular monitoring
and analysis of receivable accounts and start the process of writing off the accounts
in conformity with COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that management should impose substantial amount
compliance to GSIS billings on loans through prioritization in the salary deductions
of employees who have outstanding loans. The Agency Authorized Officer (AAO)
should make sure that before approving the loan applied by a particular employee,
his/her net take home pay is sufficient to cover the regular monthly amortization.

We recommended and management agreed to revisit the provisions of the
above cited guidelines. Describe appropriately the projects and activities to qualify
under the guidelines.

We recommended and management agreed to prepare the inventory list of
items procured and/or donated for DRRM and submit the same to COA and
Municipal Accounting Office using the sample forms in COA Circular 2014-002
Annexes P-1 and P-2.3

We recommended and management agreed to expedite the construction of the
sanitary landfill in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure protection of public health
and the environment.

We recommended and they agreed that adjustment should be made on the
stale checks to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to optimally utilize the available resources
to achieve maximum disaster preparedness.

We recommended that management give similar effort in the prosecution of
NGAs and locally funded projects.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:
a. Require the MAO and GSO to maintain sufficient property ledger cards
and property stock cards, respectively, as control records to facilitate the
regular reconciliation of records with MAO;
b. Determine and dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties in
accordance with the guidelines;
c. Provide depreciation for the Land Improvements and Infrastructure
Assets; and
d. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to conduct regular monitoring
and analysis of receivable accounts and start the process of writing off the accounts
in conformity with COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that management should impose substantial amount
compliance to GSIS billings on loans through prioritization in the salary deductions
of employees who have outstanding loans. The Agency Authorized Officer (AAO)
should make sure that before approving the loan applied by a particular employee,
his/her net take home pay is sufficient to cover the regular monthly amortization.

We recommended and management agreed to revisit the provisions of the
above cited guidelines. Describe appropriately the projects and activities to qualify
under the guidelines.

We recommended and management agreed to prepare the inventory list of
items procured and/or donated for DRRM and submit the same to COA and
Municipal Accounting Office using the sample forms in COA Circular 2014-002
Annexes P-1 and P-2.3

We recommended and management agreed to expedite the construction of the
sanitary landfill in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure protection of public health
and the environment.

We recommended and they agreed that adjustment should be made on the
stale checks to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to optimally utilize the available
resources to achieve maximum disaster preparedness.

We recommended that management give similar effort in the prosecution of
NGAs and locally funded projects.

Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided. Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.

Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and

Require the MAO and GSO to maintain ledger cards and property cards, respectively, as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records with MAO; and d. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.reason: Due from NGO's/PO's amounting to P215,000.00 still not returned.

observations.recommendation: All the planned programs, projects and activities incorporated in the LGU’s budget should be included in the Annual Investment Program.  Revisit the guidelines set on the harmonization of local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting, and expenditure management in the DBM-NEDA-DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007.

All programs and activities that are to be implemented by the LGU should contain specifics as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken.  Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided.  Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.
Record the purchases of supplies and materials held for consumption under inventory account and monitor its receipt and utilization.

Prioritize programs, projects and activities in the ensuing year specifically those mentioned in Section 100 (c) and 272 of RA 7160 to address the problems of the students and teachers particularly on school facilities.
Periodically monitor the balances of appropriations of the fund and relate the same to its targeted work and financial plan in order to implement those programs, projects and activities as scheduled to avoid accumulation of balances in which the purpose have already been satisfied.
